structure: Level 0 abstract (~2,000 tokens). Domain details in .claude/stack/{domain}.json. Cross-cutting details in .claude/stack/shared.json. Load only what the current task requires.
philosophy: Use as little AI as possible — combine deterministic logic with LLMs.
Ship small, iterate fast. Proof of concept before polish.
Every layer should work independently before integrating with the next.
Code is a commodity — protect structural integrity, not the craft of writing it.
Treat AI-generated code as technical debt until simplified.
Build from first principles — own and understand your foundation.
Natural language is the highest abstraction layer of programming.

engineering_methodology.role: system-governor — success is measured by architectural intent precision, not syntax proficiency
pillars: component-thinking: govern interconnections, data flows, and failure modes
full-stack-oversight: direct the entire stack to eliminate human bottlenecks
communication-clarity: vague instructions yield broken code — provide WHY, not just WHAT
ruthless-simplification: remove every line that doesn't break the test suite
systematic-testing: reject AI optimism — the LLM will write passing tests that fail in production
engineering_methodology.details: Full methodology (simplification loop, parallel agency, communication checklist) in stack/shared.json

principles: Integration tests hit a real database — mocks hide real bugs.
Every production failure becomes a Level 1 unit test.
CI blocks merge on test failure — no exceptions.
testing.evaluation_hierarchy: Level 1 (unit tests, every change) → Level 2 (LLM-as-Judge + human, bi-weekly) → Level 3 (AB testing, major releases). Details in stack/shared.json
testing.ai_optimism_defense: Reject tests that mock away the logic being validated. Ask 'What could really go wrong here?'

cross_cutting.hitl: Automate the routine 50%, gate the tricky 50% behind human judgment. Three pillars: deferred execution, state serialization, stateless resume. Details in stack/shared.json
cross_cutting.context_engineering: Curate the smallest set of high-signal tokens. Prune, summarize, rank. Never treat the context window as infinite. Details in stack/shared.json
cross_cutting.mcp: Expose Python functions as MCP tools for modular, composable capabilities. Server → service → utility layers. Details in stack/shared.json
cross_cutting.blueprint_to_production: Vision → stack justification → pipeline architecture → persistence → engineering → deployment → productization. Details in stack/shared.json

backend.stack: Python 3.12+, FastAPI, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL

frontend.stack: TypeScript, React 18+, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Zustand

ai.stack: LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse, structured outputs, context engineering, multi-stage pipelines

ai_rag.stack: Hybrid RAG (BM25 + vectors + RRF + cross-encoder), retrieval paradigms, index persistence, NDCG@10 evaluation

ai_eval.stack: LLM-as-a-Judge, HITL alignment protocol, meta-prompting, failure mode collection

infrastructure.stack: Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx, GitHub Actions, VPS, Grafana, Celery Beat

security.stack: JWT + refresh tokens, bcrypt, rate limiting, CORS, webhook verification, MCP permissions

product.stack: Discovery protocol, sprint model, scoping, accuracy paradigm, delivery, productization path
